clusters.theme: 3D Immersive Technologies for Educational Game Development
clusters.description: This cluster represents the intersection of immersive technologies (VR/AR) with educational game development using Unity3D as the primary tool.

clusters.theme: General Game Development with Motivational Design
clusters.description: This cluster focuses on general game development practices emphasizing gamification and player motivation.
A focused cluster on creating educational games using the Unity game engine.
This cluster emphasizes the design aspects of game-based learning approaches.
This cluster represents the technical aspects of AI implementation in Unity-based games.

discussion_points.title: Cluster 1 (Red): 3D Immersive Technologies for Educational Game Development
discussion_points.content: This cluster represents the intersection of immersive technologies (VR/AR) with educational game development using Unity3D as the primary tool.

discussion_points.title: Cluster 2 (Green): General Game Development with Motivational Design
discussion_points.content: This cluster focuses on general game development practices emphasizing gamification and player motivation.

discussion_points.title: Cluster 3 (Blue): Educational Game Development with Unity
discussion_points.content: A focused cluster on creating educational games using the Unity game engine.

discussion_points.title: Cluster 4 (Yellow): Game Design for Learning
discussion_points.content: This cluster emphasizes the design aspects of game-based learning approaches.

discussion_points.title: Cluster 5 (Purple): AI Integration in Game Engines
discussion_points.content: This cluster represents the technical aspects of AI implementation in Unity-based games.

discussion_points.title: Bibliometric Methodology
discussion_points.content: The research methodology was developed through iterative discussions emphasizing reproducibility and systematic approaches to literature analysis.

discussion_points.title: Key Themes in Game Development Education
discussion_points.content: The bibliometric analysis reveals that game development education research centers around Unity-based development, immersive technologies (VR/AR), and gamification as key pedagogical approaches.
